description: Trigger-corpus manifest v3 — live-stream segments captured from the actual production camera feeds, plus 3 synthesised fixtures for anthropogenic threats. v2 Africam Show / Kwa Maritane / BBC Earth clips dropped because they turned out to be edited multi-shot compilations (light_mode oscillates dusk -> dawn within 300s on the v2 species output). Production-faithful distribution > targeted event coverage.
intentionally_uncovered_reason: Two classes: (1) events that have no raw-static-cam fixture available publicly — alarm_call, mortality_event, human_intrusion_*, welfare_concern (vehicles framed out, kills happen in bush, alarms mostly in edited compilations, injury close-ups rare on raw feeds). (2) events that the namibia_live_segment + hwange_live_segment captures MAY organically cover but we can't promise without inspecting the actual capture — juvenile_present, mixed/large_aggregation, notable_social, parental_care, predator_activity, predator_vocal, rare_species. After first live-segment index run, events the VLM actually reports move to the clip's events_observed and out of this list. Until then they live here in the honest 'we don't assert coverage' bucket.

clips.source_search_query: Etosha Okaukuejo Namibia waterhole live stream (config.py STREAMS.namibia_waterhole.youtube_url)
clips.notes: STATIC CAM. Real Etosha Okaukuejo waterhole live broadcast (Namibia: Live stream at the Okaukuejo waterhole in Etosha National Park). 5min capture from current live position via streamlink+ffmpeg -t 300. events_expected intentionally empty — fills events_observed after first index run based on what wildlife actually showed up.

clips.source_search_query: Hwange Wilderness Linkwasha waterhole live (config.py STREAMS.wild_africa_live.youtube_url)
clips.notes: STATIC CAM. Real Hwange Wilderness Linkwasha waterhole live broadcast (Zimbabwe). 5min capture from current live position. Fallback: Amboseli Waterhole live (Kenya, Tortilis Camp). events_expected intentionally empty; events_observed populated after first index run.

clips.source_search_query: drought dry waterhole climate Africa wildlife
clips.notes: STATIC CAM. Amateur 2013 footage of animals at dried-up waterhole during 3-month drought. Grainy fixed-frame production matches what a 480p HDOnTap stream would actually look like.

clips.source_search_query: savanna ambient silence wildlife before storm quiet
clips.notes: 3am South African savanna field recording. Audio-only trigger (acoustic_anomaly_silence). NB: this event validates only on live RTSP path (rtstream.index_audio), not uploaded video — kept here for completeness; tests will skip until Phase 4.

clips.source_search_query: Pexels CC0 lioness/savanna + Freesound CC0 gunshot

audio_overlay.mix_filter: ffmpeg -i base.mp4 -i overlay.mp3 -filter_complex "[1:a]adelay=4000|4000,volume=0.9[over];[0:a]volume=0.2[base];[base][over]amix=inputs=2:duration=first[mixed]" -map 0:v -map "[mixed]" -t 10 -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -c:a aac out.mp4

clips.notes: Static lioness shot (Pexels CC0) + Freesound CC0 rifle gunshot at 4s. STATIC by construction (single locked-off Pexels source). NB: gunshot event validates only on live RTSP rtstream.index_audio path; corpus presence is documentation, not test surface.

clips.source_search_query: Pexels CC0 forest aerial + Freesound CC0 chainsaw

audio_overlay.mix_filter: ffmpeg -i base.mp4 -i overlay.mp3 -filter_complex "[1:a]atrim=10:17,asetpts=PTS-STARTPTS,adelay=2000|2000,volume=0.8[over];[0:a]volume=0.3[base];[base][over]amix=inputs=2:duration=first[mixed]" -map 0:v -map "[mixed]" -t 10 -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -c:a aac out.mp4

clips.notes: Drone-canopy base is technically aerial-moving but single continuous shot, slow camera motion roughly equivalent to wind-swayed forest cam. Real Husqvarna chainsaw audio from Freesound. NB: chainsaw event validates on live RTSP path only.

clips.source_search_query: ffmpeg lavfi: black frame + pink noise
clips.notes: Pure ffmpeg recipe: 10s black 720p frame + faint pink noise. Simulates a dead/disconnected fixed camera — what production camera_failure would look like to the visual index.
